mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate lowers risk-free discount rates, boosting present values of future earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward instances where FEDFUNDS declines are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6 percent signals excess labor market slack that erodes household income growth and compresses corporate revenue expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 6%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in retail sales and industrial production produce a demand-supply double squeeze that amplifies corporate earnings downside, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change negative and INDPRO 3-month change negative
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a federal funds rate above 4.5% restricts credit availability and raises debt service costs, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as liquidity contractions propagate.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50% of weeks when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in industrial production exceeding 1 percent signals broad-based inventory liquidation and factory gate pricing pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a 1% monthly drop in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the unemployment rate and the federal funds rate force equity multiple compression and downward earnings revisions through collapsing demand and rising discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises month-over-month and FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint increases in UNRATE and FEDFUNDS fail to produce negative 5-day returns in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1400 thousand units indicate deficient residential investment momentum, which drags construction employment and durable goods consumption lower and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1400.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarterly growth in real GDP signals an economy-wide contraction in aggregate demand that reduces corporate top-line revenues and profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative GDPC1 growth is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample quarters.
